yafu_siqs_baseline.scaling_argument: SIQS is L[1/2, 1+o(1)] = exp((1+o(1)) * sqrt(ln N * ln ln N)). For each +10 digits, time multiplies by ~8-10x. This is the best known single-core performance for numbers under 100 digits. Any novel approach should aim for a slower growth rate — e.g. L[1/3] grows ~3-4x per +10 digits, L[1/4] would grow ~2x.
